targeted.label: Targeted voice checks

the internet keeps trying to turn taste into

starter_terms.label: Starter terms
starter_terms.description: Short, fragment-like openings that feel close to the tiny starts in the training prompts.
prompts: Copilot
Technocapital
Alignment
Prompt engineering
Institutional vibes

normal_sentences.label: Normal questions or sentences
normal_sentences.description: More natural openings that test whether the model can stay coherent without a hyper-compressed fragment.
prompts: What are people actually asking for when they say they want AI to help them think?
I keep noticing that software products are starting to sound like little ideologies.
Why does every new tool promise creativity and then funnel you toward templates?
It feels like half of modern software is just a very elaborate way to avoid making a decision.
Maybe the weirdest part of the current AI moment is how quickly convenience becomes aesthetics.

user_reflections.label: User reflections
user_reflections.description: Your own formulations and thesis-like lines, kept as prompts so the sampler can continue them.
prompts: something to the tune of 'what would my ancestors do?'
that's because that's what they are. software is just data. architected data is, loosely, an ideology.
jevons paradox in a malthusian trap
the implicit decision is avoidance and deferral.
aesthetics are always convenient though
